name: Securonix API
what_it_does: Next-generation SIEM and UEBA platform providing behavioral analytics, threat hunting, insider threat detection, and SOAR integration via REST API.
use_cases: Automate threat hunting by querying behavioral anomaly scores via API
Integrate SOC automation agents with incident and violation workflows
Pull user and entity risk scores into downstream decision systems
Trigger playbooks and SOAR actions programmatically from agent pipelines
Correlate log telemetry with behavioral baselines for insider threat detection
not_for: Small or mid-market organizations — requires enterprise budget and implementation resources
Quick-start integrations — onboarding and API access require vendor engagement
best_when: Best when building SOC automation agents that need to query a mature behavioral analytics platform with rich user/entity risk context.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need rapid self-serve API access, a free tier for prototyping, or simple log search without behavioral analytics.

auth.notes: Token-based authentication; OAuth 2.0 supported in newer releases. Enterprise deployments typically use service account tokens.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise contract only; pricing based on data volume and user count. No public pricing.
pricing.notes: Requires sales engagement and enterprise contract. No self-serve trial or free tier available.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits are configurable per deployment; typically governed by enterprise SLA rather than hard API caps

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API errors are functional but documentation on error codes is sparse; enterprise support fills gaps.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent; write/action operations (e.g., closing violations) are not always safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: API documentation is gated behind customer portal — not publicly accessible
No public SDK means agents must implement raw HTTP calls with custom retry logic
Session tokens expire and require refresh logic in long-running agent processes
Some endpoints have undocumented rate limits that surface only under load

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP authorized deployment available. SOC2 and ISO27001 certified. Enterprise-grade role-based access controls.
